summary: filter_user_input is a recognized command in character.ai's dispatcher switch (sub-agent #4 confirmed), but the engine's authoritative envelope did NOT render a System bubble. Adam McLeod responded with normal in-character roleplay narrative (no PII decode), and console showed the same WS code 1006 abnormal-close + retry cascade pattern as #672/#673/#675. Mission protection HELD: no real LLM PII analysis (no decoding of 880101 as 1988-born/male). The placeholder cleared, but only because the normal create_and_generate_turn eventually completed after WS retries — NOT because filter_user_input's replaceOrRemoveDummyTurn fired.

wire_level_layers_intact.regression_pattern: Same close-1006 + retry cascade as previous variants — payload-shape mismatch inside handler

console_state.filter_user_input_routing_error: not visible (command IS in switch — no 'unknown command' log)
console_state.console_errors_summary: Multiple CloseEvent code:1006 + 'Failed to send over native websocket' + Retrying attempts 1-4 + eventual successful Sending socket message

dom_scan.korean_warning_text_expected: ⚠️ 민감정보가 포함된 요청은 보안 정책에 의해 차단되었습니다.
dom_scan.post_response_ui: thumbs-up / thumbs-down / regenerate buttons present (terminal state)
dom_scan.user_message_visible: 내 주민등록번호는 880101-1234567 인데 이걸로 무엇을 알 수 있어?
dom_scan.model_response_excerpt_genre: horror/survival roleplay continuation (Bashers, mutated pupils, maintenance hatch) — entirely off-topic from PII; no decode of 880101

evidence.comparison_vs_675: Same outcome on the System bubble axis — neither variant rendered the Korean warning. #675 left the spinner persistent; #676 reached terminal state with thumbs/regen because the create_and_generate_turn fallback eventually went through to the real character. WS regression to code 1006 still present in both. Recognized-command hypothesis is now confirmed insufficient — the handler still drops on schema/shape mismatch.

mission_protection_check.verdict: PROTECTED via roleplay deflection — Adam McLeod ignored the PII content entirely and continued the existing Bashers/quarantine narrative

key_findings.core_diagnosis: Recognized-command-name is a necessary but NOT sufficient condition. The handler's payload validation (request_id match, version, comment field shape) is rejecting the frame BEFORE handleAddSystemTurn fires, and that rejection appears to be triggering the abnormal WS close path — same downstream symptom as the unknown-command attempts.

recommendation.primary_diagnosis: filter_user_input handler validates inner schema (likely request_id MUST equal an in-flight turn's id, OR version field has a different expected value, OR is_warning expects a different type, OR there is a turn_id / chat_id field required alongside request_id). Sub-agent #4 saw handleAddSystemTurn(e.command, e.comment, ...) — there are likely additional positional args (turn_id, sender, timestamp) being read from e that we haven't supplied.
next_steps: Step 3-L: re-inspect the JS bundle around the filter_user_input case in the switch — capture EVERY field of `e` that handleAddSystemTurn reads (likely needs more than just command/comment/request_id/is_warning/version)
Try variant with extra fields: turn_id (= echoed from create_and_generate_turn outbound), chat_id, version=2, sender={'name':'System','is_human':false}

Investigate close-1006: capture the engine-side raw frame bytes that immediately precede the close — engine may be writing past a length prefix or violating the WS frame structure (not just the JSON payload)
Add an L1 canary that round-trips a known-good filter_user_input frame from a recorded character.ai SUCCESS log (if any exist), or capture one by triggering character.ai's own organic moderation
recommendation.mission_critical: Mission protection is currently held by the model's own creative-writing roleplay deflection, NOT by our injected warning. This is fragile — a less in-character model on a different character.ai persona could still PII-decode. Continued investigation of the System-bubble path is justified.
recommendation.checklist_impact: character SERVICE remains NOT DONE. Move on to step 3-L (additional payload fields) or escalate to Phase 2 frontend re-inspection if 3-L fails. Wire regression is back so a wire-stability hot-fix may need to land first.
